title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Another Major Country Bans Children From Social Media
stories.summary:
Another Major Country Bans Children From Social Media. French lawmakers voted Tuesday to ban social media for children under 15, making France the first European Union country to adopt such a restriction and adding momentum to a growing global push to protect minors online.

Both chambers of Parliament approved the sweeping ban, delivering a significant policy victory to French President Emmanuel Macron. The ...

Al Jazeera: France becomes first EU country to officially ban social media for children. Al Jazeera reports that france will ban social media for under-15s from September 1, following the UK and Australia’s moves toward blanket bans.

links.excerpt: France will ban social media for under-15s from September 1, following the UK and Australia’s moves toward blanket bans.

Al Jazeera reports that the Lebanese army has begun deploying in three southern towns as part of a US-backed framework agreement with Israel.

stories.title: Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify
stories.summary:
Ten killed as Russian attacks on merchant ships in Black Sea intensify. Russian strikes on the Odesa region have killed 28 people in July alone, local authorities say.

Al Jazeera: Daytime Russian strike kills three in Zaporizhzhia.

Al Jazeera reports that a Russian guided aerial bomb attack on Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, killed at least three people and injured 13 others.

stories.title: RFK Punishes Blue States For Rampant Fraud Concerns
stories.summary:
RFK Punishes Blue States For Rampant Fraud Concerns. The Trump administration is withholding more than $1 billion in federal Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota while demanding the Democrat-led states prove questioned Medicaid claims were lawful and not the result of waste, fraud, or abuse.

Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced Tuesday that the Department of Health and Human ...

Breitbart: RFK Jr. Freezes $1B in Medicaid to California, Minnesota over Fraud Fears.

Breitbart reports that health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced Tuesday that the Trump administration is deferring more than $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Democrat-led California and Minnesota over high-risk claims flagged in a crackdown on fraud.

The post RFK Jr. Freezes $1B in Medicaid to California, Minnesota over Fraud Fears</.

stories.title: The Shocking Payments To Dead People Trump Just Stopped
stories.summary:
The Shocking Payments To Dead People Trump Just Stopped. The Treasury Department said Tuesday that it prevented nearly $100 million in welfare payments to dead people, thanks to new fraud prevention measures implemented by the Trump administration.

Since March 2025, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the department had identified 4,900 payments worth $99 million that were slated to go to dead people. The payments ...

Breitbart: Treasury Department Stops Nearly $100M in Payments to Dead People. Breitbart reports that the U.S. Treasury Department stopped nearly $100 million in federal payments that have been going out to deceased individuals after establishing a government-wide payment verification process last year.

The post Treasury Department Stops Nearly $100M in Payments to Dead People appeared first on Bre.

stories.title: The Odyssey was filmed on Imax film. Only one southern hemisphere cinema can screen it – video
stories.summary:
The Odyssey was filmed on Imax film. Only one southern hemisphere cinema can screen it – video.

Christopher Nolan's latest film, an adaptation of Homer's epic The Odyssey, is the the first feature film ever shot entirely on Imax 1570 film, the highest-resolution film format in existence. There are now only 41 cinemas in the world capable of projecting the format.

Guardian Australia goes behind the scenes at Imax Melbourne, the only cinema in the southern hemisphere with a 1570 reel of Nolan's film <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/film/2026/jul/14/christopher-nolan-fans.

stories.title: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
stories.summary:
AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app. Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks.

Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.

links.headline: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
links.excerpt: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube, and TikTok to become all-purpose entertainment destinations instead.
links.imageAlt: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app

The Verge: Apple’s rumored ‘Upgrade’ program brings lease-to-own pricing for iPhones, Macs, and iPads.

stories.title: How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table
stories.summary:
How AI may drive union-resistant tech workers to the bargaining table.

Tech workers are increasingly unionizing, trading Silicon Valley’s myth of exceptionalism for collective bargaining to contest the corporate deployment of artificial intelligence For decades, the technology industry was a fortress that labor unions couldn’t breach.

Tech workers already had cushy compensation packages, dream benefits like unlimited vacation and free lunch, and a flat corporate hierarchy that made engineers feel as powerful as their bosses, all of whom dressed down in sne.

stories.summary:
Headaches for Silicon Valley as China chips away at the US’s lead in the AI race. Google’s AI struggles scream trouble as new Chinese models (again) throw US tech dominance into question Hello, I’m Blake Montgomery, writing to you after a double-header feature of Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey and the World Cup final.

stories.title: Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’
stories.summary:
Election voting advice from AI chatbots ‘inaccurate and unreliable’.

Research during Hungary election shows AI recommended parties not running and gave highly volatile answers to identical prompts AI chatbots provide inaccurate, inconsistent and unreliable guidance to voters asking which party they should back, a study suggests, often recommending the wrong party, not mentioning the right one, or listing parties not even running.

“The results raise serious concerns about the reliability of general-purpose AI systems in electoral contexts,”<a href=".

stories.title: Olongapo solar project sparks environmental, IP rights concerns
stories.summary:
Olongapo solar project sparks environmental, IP rights concerns.

OLONGAPO CITY—An environmental investigative mission conducted by scientists, engineers, church groups, and indigenous Filipino advocates has raised concerns over the proposed expansion of the Olongapo Solar Power Project, warning of possible impacts on biodiversity, water resources, livelihoods, and the rights of Aeta communities.

The findings, released on Tuesday, came from a three-day Environmental Investigative Mission.

stories.title: Marcoleta pleads not guilty to plunder, 1 of 3 ‘gift’ raps
stories.summary:
Marcoleta pleads not guilty to plunder, 1 of 3 ‘gift’ raps. MANILA, Philippines — Sen. Rodante Marcoleta on Wednesday pleaded not guilty to charges of plunder and violating a presidential decree that prohibits government officials from receiving, and private individuals from conferring, gifts in the Sandiganbayan.

The anti-graft court’s Third Division is handling the plunder case of Marcoleta, his donors former Rep. Mike Defensor and businessmen, Joseph Espiritu and Aristotle. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Groups to Cayetano: Marcoleta, Estrada not political prisoners.

stories.title: Barangay health workers urge Marcos: Prioritize Magna Carta before Sona
stories.summary:
Barangay health workers urge Marcos: Prioritize Magna Carta before Sona.

MANILA, Philippines — The National Confederation of Barangay Health Workers of the Philippines (NCBHWP) and UNI Global Union-Philippine Liaison Council (UNI-PLC), representing the country’s 277,000 barangay health workers (BHWs), requested President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to prioritize the passage of the long-delayed Magna Carta of BHWs.

The coalition hand-delivered their letter to Malacañang on Tuesday to renew.

stories.title: Marcos distributes assistance in Bangsamoro region
stories.summary:

stories.title: Taal Volcano logs increased seismic activity, 2 eruptions in 24 hours
stories.summary:
Taal Volcano logs increased seismic activity, 2 eruptions in 24 hours.

LUCENA CITY — Taal Volcano in Batangas province displayed signs of volcanic unrest after recording increased seismic activity and two short-lived phreatomagmatic eruptions over the past 24 hours, the Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (Phivolcs) reported on Wednesday.

In its Wednesday (July 22) morning bulletin, Phivolcs said the volcano registered 10 volcanic earthquakes and.

stories.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
stories.summary: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children. Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

stories.title: Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says
stories.summary:
Not enough water for UK’s datacentre plans, trade body says. Industry says government’s failure to address cooling demands means AI growth plans are ‘fatally flawed’ The UK will not have enough water for future datacentres, the water industry has said in stark criticism of the government’s AI growth plans.

Datacentres rely on large amounts of water to manage the heat generated by densely packed servers. Cooling towers, chillers and humidification systems make direct use of this water, with large volumes also consumed indirectly through high.
